g.text: Plants convert sunlight into food and oxygen
Happens inside chloroplasts — the green organelles in plant cells
all three ingredients meet inside the chloroplast
Without any one of these three, photosynthesis stops
Light splits water → releases O₂ and stores energy as ATP/NADPH
CO₂ + ATP/NADPH → glucose (C₆H₁₂O₆) — no light needed
Limiting factors — any one can slow or stop photosynthesis
Optimal temp: 25–30°C | High CO₂ boosts rate most
Thylakoids = light reactions | Stroma = Calvin cycle
● Limiting factors: light, CO₂, temperature, water
● O₂ is a by-product — the air we breathe!
● Chlorophyll absorbs red + blue light, reflects green
